fields.description:
# PR posture on the GitHub mirror: welcome-bot redirect + shepherded-patch intake (with pre-flight experiments)

## Why

PRs opened against the mirror can never merge (today a repository ruleset rejects merges; PRs would sit open and unanswered — the posture universally condemned across peers: Gerrit's own mirror with rotting PRs, pre-archive AOSP). Peers split: Chromium/Qt/torvalds flipped GitHub's native Feb-2026 `has_pull_requests: false` setting (zero-cost but silent); LibreOffice/Wikimedia/OpenDev bot-close with a warm redirect. For a project that wants to convert drive-bys into repeat contributors, research recommends keeping PRs ENABLED as an intake channel: a bot answers instantly and the maintainer converts worthwhile patches to Gerrit (Wikimedia's "ask someone to convert" path).

## What

Decision (record as ADR `docs/adr/0037-oss-contribution-intake-posture.md`, covering: PR posture chosen + rejected alternatives incl. native disable and GerritBot-style auto-import; revisit trigger = PR volume exceeding solo-shepherd capacity): keep PRs ENABLED + auto-answer bot.

- Add `.github/workflows/lockdown.yml` using dessant/repo-lockdown **SHA-pinned with a trailing version comment** (repo convention per gerrit-verify.yaml: "All third-party actions are SHA-pinned"; resolve the current v5.x release SHA at execution, e.g. `dessant/repo-lockdown@<sha> # v5.0.2`). Trigger: `pull_request_target: types: [opened, reopened]` (`reopened` closes Wikimedia's reopen loophole, T374157). Explicit least-privilege block: `permissions: { pull-requests: write }` (nothing else — `pull_request_target` runs with an elevated token on fork PRs; issues are real intake here, so the workflow must NOT touch issues: no `issues:` events, no issue inputs). `close-pr: true`, `lock-pr: false` (LibreOffice pattern — thread stays open). **Exempt `dependabot[bot]`** (job-level `if: github.actor != 'dependabot[bot]'`): the release-hygiene story configures Dependabot whose PRs are advisory notifications that a maintainer converts to Gerrit changes — the bot must not close them. Verify the v5 input key names against the action's manifest (action.yml) at execution; a renamed input fails silently.
- Comment message per the convergent five-part skeleton: thank by name; "read-only mirror, PRs can't merge here — this close is automatic, nothing is wrong with your change"; deep-link Gerrit (GitHub OAuth sign-in) + `docs/your-first-change.md` (exists before this lands — this story depends_on the onboarding story); shepherded-patch escape hatch; warm tone.
- Publish the shepherded-patch policy in CONTRIBUTING.md (new section; Qt's voice — allowed, explicitly slower, best-effort): contributor runs `git commit -s` (their own DCO sign-off — required; see the DCO section) then attaches `git format-patch -1` output to a GitHub issue (plain diff acceptable with name/email to credit); maintainer applies with `git am` (author = contributor, committer = shepherd), adds their own `Signed-off-by` and the `rebar-ticket:` trailer via amend (message-only; authorship untouched), pushes `refs/for/main`, drives review; `Co-authored-by:` when substantially rewritten. If E2 (below) finds Forge Author NOT grantable, the policy text instead promises `Co-authored-by:` credit — the section is written after E2 resolves.
- Update the GitHub repo description: "Read-only mirror — code review at https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com".

Pre-flight experiments (complete BEFORE the workflow lands; record each outcome as a ticket comment AND fold consequences into the affected artifact):
- [E1] Scratch-repo test of `has_pull_requests: false` fork-side UX — documents the rejected alternative in the ADR.
- [E2] Admin read of Gerrit ACLs (JoeOakhartNava identity) on the live server + `infra/gerrit/project.config`: is Forge Author granted to Registered Users on the rebar project? If not, add the grant via the declarative config path or downgrade the authorship promise (go/no-go feeds the policy text above).
- [E3] Audit ALL `.github/workflows/*` for `pull_request`/`pull_request_target` event dependencies — known set today: test.yml, optionality.yml, eval.yml, eval-plan-review-container.yml (enumerate at execution with `grep -l "pull_request" .github/workflows/*`); confirm none is broken by bot-closed fork PRs (they run on PR events but fork PRs being closed quickly may cancel runs — acceptable; record). Any real breakage found is fixed in this story or ticketed via discovered_from.
- [E4] Confirm the `rebar-ticket:` trailer policy for shepherded patches (default: shepherd creates/uses a ticket and amends the trailer in) and cross-check it against the onboarding tutorial's outsider-ticket flow.

Note: maintainer-opened test PRs are NOT exempted from the bot — a closed test PR is precisely the verification (AC1); no exemption logic.

## Scope

IN: lockdown workflow + message text, CONTRIBUTING.md shepherded-patch section, repo description, ADR 0037, experiments E1–E4.
OUT: GerritBot-style automatic PR→Gerrit import — non-goal recorded in ADR 0037: only Go operates one; the gerrit github-pullrequest plugin is dormant; even at Go's scale bridged PRs merge at ~34% vs ~78% native (golang discussion #61182). Also OUT: issue lockdown (Issues are real intake), changes to the merge-blocking ruleset.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A test PR from a non-collaborator fork receives the comment and is closed (not locked) — prove: the test PR's timeline shows the bot comment + closed state; a REOPENED test PR is re-closed (covers the `reopened` type).
- [ ] Message content check — prove by grep of the workflow file: contains "read-only mirror", "nothing is wrong with your change", a link to `docs/your-first-change.md`, a link to https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com, and the patch-fallback offer; contains none of "rejected", "not accepted", "closed as invalid".
- [ ] Workflow hardening — prove: `lockdown.yml` has a `permissions:` block granting only `pull-requests: write`; the action ref matches `@[0-9a-f]{40} # v` (SHA-pinned); input keys verified against the pinned action.yml.
- [ ] Dependabot exemption present — prove: `grep -q "dependabot" .github/workflows/lockdown.yml`; a Dependabot-authored PR (or a simulated actor test) is NOT closed by the bot.
- [ ] E1–E4 outcomes recorded as ticket comments; E2's go/no-go reflected in the CONTRIBUTING policy text; E3 breakage (if any) fixed or ticketed.
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md shepherded-patch section present with all four required elements — prove, each individually against the section: `grep -q "format-patch" CONTRIBUTING.md`; `grep -q "Signed-off-by" CONTRIBUTING.md` (both-trailer DCO handling); `grep -qi "author" CONTRIBUTING.md` reflecting the E2 outcome (either `git am` authorship preservation or `Co-authored-by` fallback — whichever E2 selected); `grep -q "rebar-ticket" CONTRIBUTING.md` (trailer policy); `grep -qi "best-effort" CONTRIBUTING.md` (caveat).
- [ ] ADR 0037 committed covering posture decision, native-disable rejection (E1 evidence), GerritBot non-goal, revisit trigger. Repo description updated — prove: `gh api repos/navapbc/rebar --jq .description` contains "read-only" or "mirror".
